steps.prompt: How many verbs does this sentence contain?

hints.One: Each 'non' negates a separate verb — there are three: 'feram, patiar, sinam'
hints.Two: Count again — 'feram, patiar, sinam' are three distinct verbs
hints.Four: There are three verbs, each preceded by 'non' — 'feram, patiar, sinam'

hints.to carry: 'to carry' is close — 'ferre' means both 'to carry' and 'to bear/endure'; in this context, 'endure' fits
hints.to strike: 'to strike' is 'ferire' — similar but different from 'ferre'

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive of 'fero' is also 'feram' — but the emphatic context suggests future indicative (a firm promise)
hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'fero' — 'feram' is future
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'ferrem' — 'feram' is future indicative

hints.ferio, ferire, —, —: These are the (defective) parts of 'ferire' (to strike) — different from 'ferre'
hints.facio, facere, feci, factum: These are the parts of 'facere' (to do/make)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)

hints.to open: 'to open' is 'patere' (to lie open) — 'pati' is a deponent meaning 'to suffer/endure/allow'

hints.pateo, patere, patui, —: These are the parts of 'patere' (to lie open/be exposed) — a different verb
hints.paro, parare, paravi, paratum: These are the parts of 'parare' (to prepare)
hints.pareo, parere, parui, paritum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to obey)

hints.scio, scire, scivi, scitum: These are the parts of 'scire' (to know)
hints.sero, serere, sevi, satum: These are the parts of 'serere' (to sow/plant)
hints.sisto, sistere, stiti, statum: These are the parts of 'sistere' (to halt/stop)

steps.prompt: What rhetorical figure is 'non … non … non …'?
steps.target: Anaphora with tricolon — triple repetition for emphasis
choices: Anaphora with tricolon — triple repetition for emphasis

hints.Litotes: Litotes is understatement — this is emphatic repetition of negation
hints.Chiasmus: Chiasmus reverses parallel elements — this is straightforward anaphoric repetition
hints.Asyndeton: While there are no conjunctions (asyndeton is present), the dominant figure is the triple 'non' (anaphora)

steps.prompt: What do the three verbs build toward semantically?
choices: They all mean the same thing
A descent from strong to weak

hints.They all mean the same thing: While similar, each verb escalates: 'fero' (bear), 'patior' (endure), 'sino' (allow) — from passive endurance to active permission
hints.A descent from strong to weak: The movement is from passive tolerance to active refusal of permission — an escalation
hints.Random variation for style: Cicero carefully selects synonyms in ascending order of rhetorical force

translation: I will not bear it, I will not tolerate it, I will not allow it.
